titleStmt.title: SII 40.74: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 74. (A.R. No. B. 52 of 1930.) Rēgulagaḍḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a slab lying in front of the Gopālasvāmi temple in the village.
This is dated Śaka 148[3], Raudri, Chaitra, śu. 5, Thursday, corresponding to 1561 A.D., March 21. However the week day was Friday.

Records the renovation of the temple of Kēśavanāyini at Nallapalli by Pedasūrya and also gift of 2 puṭṭis of land for providing food offering to the deity for the merit of Peddaya, when Pemmasāni Timmānāyanigāru was ruling from Rēgulagaḍḍa in Taṅgeḍa-sīma.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīmaśālivāhanaśaka
varuṣaṁbulu 1483 Agunē
ṭi raudri saṁvatsara caitra śu 5 guruvārāna
śrī vīrapratāpa śrī sadāśivadēvamahārā
julugāru pṛthivi rājyaṁ cēyucunna pemma
sāni timmānāyani gāriki pāliṁci mā
navadhariṁcina taṁgeḍa sīmalōni rēgulagaḍḍa
varicarlanulōddevu neni peddayya
gāri kumāruḍu pedasūyyagāru
nallapalli kēśavanāyini guḍipūrvāna śithila
mai Uṁḍagānu tirugabaḍi kaṭṭiṁci suṁka
vēyiṁci dēvuni Amṛtapaḷlaku tiṁḍḍi polā
na reṁḍu puḍlu coppamerapeddayyagāri
puṇyaṁgānu yistimi . śrī śrīṁ jeyunu
Digital edition of SII 40.74 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
